page: Amazon Web Services c6gd.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by a dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz with no hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the c6gd.xlarge in the average tier for stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, scoring 1519.58 and 6061.97 respectively. Large-block memory bandwidth read and write operations also perform in the average range, measuring 72.54 GB/sec and 33.66 GB/sec. However, the instance exhibits weaker performance in memory-intensive small-block reads and single-threaded gzip compression. Passmark database operations score at an average level of 2607.7, while cryptographic operations benefit from hardware-accelerated AES support.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c6gd.xlarge offers a balanced profile for workloads requiring dedicated ARM64 compute resources paired with fast local NVMe storage. The inclusion of local SSD storage without requiring external network-attached volumes reduces latency for IOPS-sensitive tasks. This configuration is suitable for multi-threaded applications, lightweight database operations, static web serving, and localized caching layers that fit within the 8.0 GB memory limit.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.xlarge is a compute-optimized, ARM64-based instance powered by a dedicated 4-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume, running on the Nitro hypervisor with a 1 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmark data indicates average performance in stress-ng CPU tests and large-block memory bandwidth, alongside average database operation capabilities. However, it shows weaker results in small-block memory reads and single-threaded compression tasks. Lacking hyperthreading and AVX extensions, this instance is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores and low-latency local storage. It is well-suited for distributed caching, lightweight database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded ARM64 applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 4 dedicated Graviton cores, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD for efficient database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated cores, 8.0 GB RAM, and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and integrated local NVMe SSD storage for efficient localized workloads.
bullet_points: AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores
8.0 GB of system memory and 237 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
Nitro hypervisor architecture with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average performance in CPU stress tests and database operations
Best suited for static web serving, caching, and lightweight databases
